mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index growth reduces real corporate earnings expectations and increases the probability of future monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If months with above-median CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth do not produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or if Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% signals severe labor market slack that crushes aggregate wage income and consumer spending, transmitting into lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If episodes where UNRATE exceeds 9.5% are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or if the mean return in triggered months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate combined with declining industrial production identifies a stagflationary monetary squeeze where financing costs rise while output contracts, causing investors to mark down earnings forecasts and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If joint periods of elevated FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO fail to underperform the linear sum of individual single-series effects, or if the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically when housing starts exceed 500k units, a regime of demand-led recovery with concurrent housing expansion; below this threshold, inventory overhang neutralizes the sales signal.
regime_hint: demand-led recovery with concurrent housing expansion
failure_condition: If positive RSAFS changes when HOUST is below 500k produce positive 20-day returns at the same frequency as when HOUST is above 500k, or if the RSAFS-return correlation is not significantly stronger in the high-HOUST regime (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect rising manufacturing sector health and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate operating leverage and profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in at least 55% of observations, or if the mean forward return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% while consumer prices are rising, deeply negative real interest rates punish cash holdings and drive capital reallocation into equities, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If triggered episodes produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or if the mean return in triggered windows is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in payroll employment and declines in the unemployment rate capture genuine labor market tightening that raises aggregate wage income and consumption expenditures more than either series alone, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If a two-factor interaction model of PAYEMS and UNRATE does not outperform the best single-factor model by at least 50% in walk-forward Rank IC over any 2-year window, or if joint signals produce positive returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Negative growth in real GDP signals a contraction in aggregate demand that reduces economy-wide corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If quarters with negative quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth are not followed by negative 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
